key.name: disable-version-validation
key.description: Allow installing extensions not marked compatible with the current GNOME Shell version

key.name: scheduled-accent-enabled
key.description: Automatically switch between day and night accent colors based on time

key.name: day-accent-color
key.description: Accent color to use during daytime hours

key.name: night-accent-color
key.description: Accent color to use during nighttime hours

key.name: shared-tinting-enabled
key.description: Use accent color tint for shell panel and dash

key.name: use-sunrise-sunset
key.description: Use automatic sunrise/sunset times instead of fixed schedule

key.name: scheduled-panel-enabled
key.description: Automatically switch panel tint color based on time of day

key.name: tb-color-button-bg-light
key.description: Per-scheme override for @button_bg_color under light colour scheme. Empty = no override.

key.name: tb-widget-input-inset
key.description: 0.0 = Adwaita-flat inputs. Higher values give entries a visible background and border so they read as clearly depressed.

key.name: tb-widget-button-raise
key.description: 0.0 = Adwaita-flat buttons. Higher values give non-flat, non-suggested buttons a border and shadow so they read as pressable.

key.name: tb-widget-headerbar-gradient
key.description: 0.0 = flat Adwaita headerbar. Higher values apply a subtle top-to-bottom gradient. Conflicts with Adwaita's flat philosophy; use sparingly.

key.name: tb-layer-headerbar-bottom
key.description: Thickness of the horizontal rule drawn under the headerbar, rendered in the Adwaita @borders colour. 0 = Libadwaita default (flush).

key.name: tb-layer-sidebar-divider
key.description: Thickness of the vertical rule between the sidebar and the main content area. 0 = Libadwaita default (blended).

key.name: tb-layer-content-contrast
key.description: Extra contrast applied to the content backdrop to visually separate it from the chrome. 0.0 = no change, 1.0 = maximum.

key.name: tb-sidebar-opacity
key.description: Controls the left-nav sidebar opacity in apps like Nautilus/Files/Disks. 1.0 = fully opaque; <1.0 lets the wallpaper or compositor blur bleed through.

key.name: tb-sidebar-fg-override
key.description: Optional #rrggbb override for sidebar text. Empty string keeps the Adwaita default.

key.name: use-wallpaper-accent
key.description: Let the experienced daemon re-extract the accent color when the wallpaper changes

key.name: tb-floating-dock-enabled
key.description: Turn the overview dash into an always-visible floating dock (requires the Dash to Dock extension)

key.name: tb-scaling-text-factor
key.description: Mirrors org.gnome.desktop.interface text-scaling-factor. 1.0 = Adwaita default. Stored on the GNOME X schema so it round-trips through Experience Packs; the infra layer writes the real GSetting on Apply.

key.name: tb-scaling-monitor-framebuffer
key.description: When true, GNOME X will add scale-monitor-framebuffer to org.gnome.mutter experimental-features. Required on Wayland for crisp fractional scaling.

key.name: tb-scaling-x11-fractional
key.description: When true, GNOME X will add x11-randr-fractional-scaling to org.gnome.mutter experimental-features. X11 counterpart of scale-monitor-framebuffer.

key.name: tb-scaling-per-app-overrides
key.description: Each entry is "APP_ID:SCALE" — e.g. "org.gnome.Nautilus:1.25". Applied on theme Apply by writing a modified ~/.local/share/applications/APP_ID.desktop. Allowed scales: 0.5, 1.0, 1.25, 1.5, 1.75, 2.0.

key.name: apply-gdm-on-theme-apply
key.summary: Also apply theme to GDM login screen (GXF-003)
key.description: When true, applying a theme also propagates the shell-theme name and accent colour to the GDM dconf database via a polkit-elevated helper. Off by default because it requires an admin password prompt. Only the name + accent are forwarded — no paths, no CSS. See docs/known-limitations.md (GDM section).

key.name: cached-wallpaper-palette
key.description: Each entry is "HEX:ACCENT_ID". The experienced daemon rewrites this whenever the wallpaper changes, so the UI can show last-known swatches without re-running extraction. Independent of use-wallpaper-accent — that gates whether we also set the accent color automatically.

key.name: wallpaper-accent-index
key.description: When use-wallpaper-accent is enabled, this governs which swatch of the cached palette drives the accent. -1 (the default) means "use the dominant colour"; 0..N-1 locks to that palette index, so when the daemon re-extracts on a wallpaper change it re-applies the accent at the same index of the new palette rather than the old hex. The UI sets this when the user clicks a wallpaper swatch and resets it to -1 when they click one of the nine stock accent buttons.

key.name: wallpaper-slideshow-name
key.description: Filesystem stem for the GNOME background XML the slideshow view writes. Defaults to "gnome-x-slideshow" so round-trips through Apply always land on the same file.

key.name: wallpaper-slideshow-pictures
key.description: Ordered list of absolute picture paths. Persisted so the UI can re-open the slideshow view and show the last configuration.

key.name: wallpaper-slideshow-interval
key.description: Hold time (seconds) per picture. Minimum 5. Default is 10 minutes.

key.name: wallpaper-slideshow-shuffle
key.description: When true, the slideshow view randomises picture order before writing the XML.
